title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Prompt Defense
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a coding-agent implementation brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.
Specify risk-proportionate tests and observable regression checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Change Scope · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Change Scope without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.

content.text: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Change Scope, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a coding-agent implementation brief.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A total rewrite is implied whenever existing code is low quality. This fails the Change Scope contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: OpenAI Codex Manual: Prompting and Best Practices (Verified current 2026-07-13) — Controls Codex prompting, Plan mode, AGENTS.md, verification, and surface guidance.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.

stimulus.title: Implementation Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A code diff is sufficient proof that a change works.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Implementation Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
